item.title: Ministry urges efficiency, better service in rural electrification
item.description: The government has called for improved efficiency, reduced system losses, and better customer service in the rural power distribution network....Geographic Reference

item.title: Month-long prints festival under way at Kala Kendra
item.description: A month-long prints festival, organised by Kala Kendra and the Kibria Printmaking Studio, is currently under way at the Kala Kendra premises in Lalmatia in the capital....

item.title: 3-day manuscript workshop begins at DU library
item.description: A three-day workshop on manuscripts began at the E-Zone of Dhaka University Library on Tuesday, aiming to promote preservation and research of historical texts....

item.title: ‘Mamun: In Praise of Shadows’ wins best documentary at UKAFF
item.description: Makbul Chowdhury’s feature-length documentary, Mamun: In Praise of Shadows, won the best documentary film award at the 28th edition of the UK Asian Film Festival, currently under way at the Garden Cinema in London’s iconic Covent Garden....

item.title: University of Canberra eyes branch campus in Bangladesh
item.description: Australia’s University of Canberra has expressed interest in establishing a branch campus in Bangladesh to expand access to international-standard higher education....

item.title: Ukraine slams Russian ‘cynicism’ ahead of separate truces
item.description: Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday accused Russia of ‘utter cynicism’ for launching deadly attacks while also seeking a truce to stage its May 9 Victory Day parade in Moscow....

item.title: ISD alumni share business insights at entrepreneurial summit
item.description: Alumni of the International School Dhaka encouraged aspiring entrepreneurs to pursue passion, embrace failure, and think long term at the ISD Alumni Entrepreneurial Summit 2026 held on April 30....

item.title: Assailants open fire on ambulance carrying bullet-hit man
item.description: Unidentified assailants opened fire on an ambulance carrying a bullet-hit man on the Dhaka–Khulna highway at the Kudir Battala point under Rupsha upazila in Khulna in the early hours of Tuesday....

item.title: Out-of-pocket spending soars to 79pc, worsens healthcare gaps: BIDS
item.description: A significant portion of Bangladesh’s population continues to face unmet healthcare needs, driven largely by rising out-of-pocket expenditures, according to a study of Bangladesh Institute of Development Studies....Country specific content

item.title: Won’t resign, defeat was a conspiracy: Mamata
item.description: TMC chief and the outgoing West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee on Tuesday ruled out stepping down after her party’s assembly poll defeat, alleging that the outcome was the result of a ‘conspiracy’ rather than the people’s mandate, reports Hindustan Times....

item.title: War hero Quazi Nooruzzaman’s 15th death anniv today
item.description: Today marks the 15th death anniversary of decorated freedom fighter Quazi Nooruzzaman, Bir Uttam, who commanded Sector 7 of the 11 sectors during the 1971 War of Independence....

item.title: ERL to resume diesel production on May 8
item.description: Eastern Refinery Limited, the lone state-owned oil refinery in Bangladesh, is expected to resume refining crude oil into diesel on May 8 after being forced to shut the plant on April 14 due to a shortage of crude oil, ERL officials said....

item.title: BNP activists snatch tender box, examines bids in Rajshahi
item.description: A group of leaders and activists of Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Swecchasebak Dal and Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Juba Dal — the volunteer and youth wings of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party — allegedly snatched and opened a tender box at the......

item.title: Local govt polls within a year, says Fakhrul
item.description: Local Government Rural Development and Cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Tuesday said that efforts would be made to hold elections at all levels of local government within the next one year....

item.title: 3 RAB members hurt in attack by drug dealers
item.description: Three members of a Rapid Action Battalion-11 intelligence team were injured in an attack by suspected drug dealers in Masdair Lichubagan area of Fatullah in Narayanganj on Tuesday afternoon....

item.title: Stocks foreign investment drops by 70pc in 5 years
item.description: Foreign investments in Bangladesh’s stock market plunged by 70 per cent over the past five years to $914.58 million at the end of December 2025, underscoring sustained capital outflows and a steady erosion of investor confidence......

item.title: Indian drone crashes along Patgram border
item.description: An Indian drone crashed into a cornfield along the Moglibari border in Burimari union under Patgram upazila in Lalmonirhat on Tuesday....

item.title: BB raises loan limit to Tk 80 lakh for EVs
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has raised loan limit to Tk 80 lakh for electric and hybrid vehicles and increased the personal loan cap to Tk 40 lakh, easing consumer financing rules while trying to steer borrowers towards greener transport......

item.title: US ready to resume combat operations
item.description: US forces are ready to resume combat operations against Iran if ordered, Washington’s top military officer said Tuesday, as the Pentagon threatened a ‘devastating’ response to Iranian attacks on commercial shipping in the Hormuz strait....

item.title: Trade deal cannot be scrapped at will: Muktadir
item.description: Commerce minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir on Tuesday said a trade agreement between states cannot be cancelled at will, stressing that such arrangements are governed by continuity and national interest......

item.title: Gold prices drop by Tk 2,158
item.description: Gold prices in Bangladesh have been reduced by Tk 2,158 per bhari, bringing the price of 22-carat gold to Tk 2,40,336, according to the Bangladesh Jewellers Association......

item.title: Job creation lags far behind workforce growth: ILO
item.description: Job creation in Bangladesh is failing to keep pace with a rapidly expanding workforce, while nearly half of workers call for short-term technical training and more than half of young people report an urgent need for digital skills, according to the......Country specific content

item.title: BB relaxes loan rules for leather traders
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has instructed banks to provide special loans to traders for purchasing and preserving rawhides ahead of the upcoming Eid-Ul-Azha, while easing repayment conditions for existing defaulters to ensure smooth financing for the sector......

item.title: Collapse of political imagination in West Bengal
item.description: WEST Bengal has long prided itself on being thepoliticalexception that refused to bend to Delhi’s dominant winds. For decades, it stood apart, cultivating a distinct rhythm of ideological stubbornness, cultural confidence, and electoral unpredictability. That illusion has now been decisively shattered. The sweeping victory of the Bharatiya Janata Party in the 2026 assembly......

item.title: What 2026 verdict shows about India’s democracy
item.description: THE results are in and the unthinkable has happened. What I had feared for the past five years has now come to pass: the Bharatiya Janata Party, under the ideological leadership of Narendra Modi, is set to form the next government in West Bengal. Suvendu Adhikari, whose record of incendiary, anti-Muslim rhetoric has dragged political discourse to new lows......

item.title: Haor farmers need more than pity
item.description: IN THE flooded fields of Sunamganj’s Pagna Haor, farmer Md Renu Mia stands knee-deep in water, staring at paddy he cannot dry, cut or sell. In Kishoreganj’s Austagram, Ratan Mia has lost his entire ten-acre crop. In Netrokona’s Khaliajuri, Palash Sarkar watches waist-deep water swallow his only harvest of the year. In Tahirpur, Ramen Biswas offered half his crop as......Politics

item.title: Measles and cracks in system
item.description: A MOTHER at Barguna Sadr Hospital waits anxiously beside a floor mattress bed as her nine-month-old baby struggles through days of high fever and rash all over her body. Though the baby could not utter her pain, her eyes showed her unendurable pain. The child’s cries have grown weaker, the family’s mental strength has nearly exhausted, and fear hangs heavy in the......

item.title: Waste-choked drains, canals signal city breakdown
item.description: THE two photographs that New Age published on May 3 and 4 lay bare a crisis where regular rainfall and unchecked waste disposal combine to paralyse city life. All this also show how drains fail and the choking of canals continues. The May 3 photograph taken at Demra shows stagnant, polluted water collecting along a road after rain, suggesting a drainage system that......

item.title: No place for partisan interests in VC appointment
item.description: THE appointment of vice-chancellors to public universities on partisan political considerations, sidestepping the relevant legislation, continues to erode the reputation of the institutions of tertiary education and degrade the quality of education and research. The University of Dhaka Order 1973, for an example, stipulates that the chancellor, the president of the country......Dailynews subscription

item.title: Saka ends Arsenal's 20-year wait to reach Champions League final
item.description: Arsenal reached the Champions League final for the first time in 20 years as Bukayo Saka sealed a 1-0 win against Atletico Madrid on Tuesday....Breaking news alert

item.title: PM’s defence adviser urges integrated approach for governance, security, dev
item.description: Prime minister’s defence adviser retired Brigadier General AKM Shamsul Islam on Tuesday underscored the need for an integrated initiative involving all state institutions to ensure good governance, national security and sustainable development....Local news updates

item.title: Home minister urges more global aid for Rohingyas
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed has called on the international community to urgently step up support for Rohingya refugees sheltered in Bangladesh, warning that declining global assistance is putting additional pressure on the humanitarian response....

item.title: Suu Kyi’s son urges Myanmar to provide ‘proof of life’
item.description: The son of Myanmar’s deposed leader Aung San Suu Kyi Tuesday demanded France’s help in seeking independently verified proof of his mother’s life after she was transferred to house arrest, her lawyers said....

item.title: Nepal PM justifies banning party-linked unions, student groups
item.description: Nepal’s newly elected prime minister Balendra Shah rejected on Tuesday criticism of his ban on trade and student unions linked topoliticalparties, insisting the move would not infringe on rights....

item.title: Farmers in Sylhet div face huge losses, sunlight brings some relief
item.description: After seven consecutive days of cloudy skies and rainfall in the Sylhet region, sunlight finally returned on Tuesday, bringing some relief to farmers in the haor belt....Diaspora community forum

item.title: Police constable held with 50,000 Yaba pills
item.description: A police constable was arrested with 50,000 Yaba tablets from the Shah Amanat bridge area of Bakalia in Chattogram on early Tuesday....

item.title: BCG detains two over Sundarban tourist attack
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard detained two miscreants in connection with an attempted attack on tourists at a resort in Sundarban....

item.title: Rohingya youth arrested over alleged TTP link
item.description: The police have arrested a Rohingya youth from Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on charges of his alleged connection with Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan, a militant organisation which mainly operates along the Afghanistan–Pakistan border....Politics

item.title: OCAB concerned over travel ban on journalist Nazrul Islam
item.description: The Overseas Correspondents Association Bangladesh on Tuesday expressed concern over the barring of journalist Nazrul Islam Mithu from travelling to China at Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka....Local news updates

item.title: Black rice cultivation sparks interest in Meherpur
item.description: A farmer’s successful cultivation of black rice in Meherpur has created new hope in the agricultural sector, as its high market value, lower production cost, and growing demand encourage local farmers to consider it as a profitable alternative crop....

item.title: 3-day IT fest to begin at Notre Dame College tomorrow
item.description: A three-day IT festival organised by the Notre Dame Information Technology Club is set to begin on Thursday at the Notre Dame College Dhaka campus in Dhaka....

item.title: Natore court orders case against complainant for misreporting child’s age
item.description: The Women and Children Repression Prevention Tribunal in Natore on Tuesday ordered the police to file a case against a complainant for misreporting a seven-year-old boy as a 21-year-old man in an attempted murder case....

item.title: JB puts Globe Janakantha assets up for auction
item.description: State-owned Janata Bank PLC has issued an auction notice for the assets of Globe Janakantha Shilpa Paribar, including the iconic 15-storey ‘Janakantha Bhaban,’ to cover for defaulted loans amounting to Tk 215 crore....Country specific content

item.title: Delegation from Kenya visits Ctg Port
item.description: A delegation comprising officials from the Kenya Ports Authority, Kenya Special Economic Zone Authority and Japan International Cooperation Agency visited the Chittagong Port Authority on Tuesday for strategic benchmarking and collaboration....

item.title: PM for conducting performance audits
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Tuesday put special emphasis on conducting performance audits along with compliance audits to ensure transparency in public expenditures....

item.title: Cost rises by 170pc before project begins
item.description: The estimated cost of the Dhaka East-West Elevated Expressway public–private partnership project has increased by 170 per cent even before the project begins, project officials said on Tuesday....
